webLinks.linkText: White Bay Power Station - Biennale of Sydney

properties.publicTransport: Route 441 and 442 buses run regularly to Rozelle. As well, route 443 heritage buses will run on weekends and public holidays. See details at transportnsw.info
properties.otherAdvice:
The Biennale of Sydney will be held at White Bay Power Station open from Tuesday-Sundays 10am-5pm daily, from Saturday 14 March to Sunday 14 June. The exhibition will also be open on public holidays.

Traffic will be heavy on roads approaching White Bay Power Station as crowds travel to and from the event,

Attendees driving should carpool with their group, plan to arrive early as local parking is limited.

Motorists should allow extra travel time on Victoria Rd, the City West Link and Robert St.

properties.publicTransport: Light rail and buses run regularly to Moore Park. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
The Sydney Family Show will be held at The Entertainment Quarter from Thursday 2 to Sunday 19 April. Show starts at 10am daily and will finish between 3pm and 5pm depending on the day.

  • Traffic is expected to be heavier than usual on Anzac Pde, Eastern Distributor, Moore Park Rd, Cleveland St and Lang Rd approaching Moore Park as families travel to and from the Show each day. Motorists should allow extra travel time in the area and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.
  • The precinct will be particularly busy while events are held at neighbouring Allianz Stadium, Sydney Cricket Ground and Hordern Pavilion so allow plenty of extra travel time as parking will be in demand on these days.
  • Driver Ave will be partially closed outside the stadiums at various times on game days on 17, 18 and 19 April.

Special event clearways will be in effect on roads in and around Moore Park on 17, 18 and 19 April (game days) while the Show takes place so check signs carefully before parking as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies.

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

arrangementElements.html:
ROAD CLOSURES

Driver Ave at Moore Park will be partially closed during events at nearby SCG and Allianz Stadium on the following dates: 

  • Friday 17 April from 3.30pm 
  • Saturday 18 April from 4.30pm 
  • Sunday 19 April from 11am

SPECIAL EVENT CLEARWAYS

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.


Special event clearways will be in place on roads in Moore Park for events at neighbouring SCG and Allianz Stadium on sections of Albion Street, Lang Road and Moore Park Road, Cleveland Street and Fitzroy Street.  

Clearways will be in effect on the following dates and times, check signs carefully to avoid being towed: 

  • Friday 17 April from 6am to midnight for the Super Rugby and AFL Derby
  • Saturday 18 April from 6am to 11pm for the A-League Sydney FC v Perth Glory
  • Sunday 19 April from 6am to 7pm for NRL Roosters v Knights
arrangementElements.title: Road Closures and Special Event Clearways

roads.mainStreet: Moore Park Road, Anzac Parade, Lang Road, Eastern Distributor

properties.publicTransport: Buses may experience delays through Mascot while closures are in place. Visit transportnsw.info for service disruption information.
properties.otherAdvice:
Anzac Day commemorations in Mascot on Sunday 19 April. A march will travel along King St from 2.15pm, finishing at Mascot Memorial Park by 2.45pm.

For the safety of the marchers, NSW Police will implement rolling road closures along Botany Rd northbound lanes from King St to Coward St from 2pm until 3pm. Sections of King St, MacIntosh St, Forster St and Coward St will also close, and there will be no access to Botany Rd northbound during this time. 

Special event clearway parking restrictions will also be in place effect from 12.30pm to 4pm on Sunday 19 April at the following locations:

  • Botany Rd between King St and Coward St (western side/northbound)
  • Coward St between Botany Rd and Henry Kendall Crescent (both sides)
  • King St between Botany Rd and approx. 90m west of Botany Rd (both sides)

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Road closures will be in place for an Anzac Day Dawn Service held outside the Brighton Le Sands RSL Club.

Bay St will be closed in both directions between The Grand Parade and Moate Ave from 5.15am to approx. 7am.

Motorists should follow the directions of traffic controllers on-site and detour around the closure via local roads.

High volumes of holiday traffic are expected approaching and through Nowra, Jervis Bay, and Milton/Ulladulla during peak holiday travel periods.

For details about known holiday traffic pinch points, see the NSW Holiday and Journey planning website and the pinch point journey planning tool.

High volumes of holiday traffic are expected approaching Heatherbrae, Hexham and towards Beresfield during peak holiday travel periods.

For details about known holiday traffic pinch points, see the NSW Holiday and Journey planning website and the pinch point journey planning tool.

High volumes of holiday traffic are expected through Lithgow during peak holiday travel periods due to the closure of Great Western Highway at Victoria Pass.

For details about known holiday traffic pinch points, see the NSW Holiday and Journey planning website and the pinch point journey planning tool.

For more information about the work we are undertaking at Victoria Pass, click here.

High volumes of holiday traffic are expected from Wahroonga to Calga on the M1 Pacific Motorway during peak holiday travel periods.

For details about known holiday traffic pinch points, see the NSW Holiday and Journey planning website and the pinch point journey planning tool.

High volumes of holiday traffic are expected approaching the NSW-Queensland border during peak holiday travel periods. You can toggle to view traffic information supplied by QLD on Live Traffic NSW. For details about known holiday traffic pinch points, see the NSW Holiday and Journey planning website and the pinch point journey planning tool.

properties.publicTransport: Race day tickets include travel on all public transport modes. L2 & L3 light rail services will not operate between Circular Quay and Central due to maintenance. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Autumn Carnival: Stakes Day at Royal Randwick Racecourse. First race at 11.25am, last race from 4.55pm.

Traffic may be heavier than usual on Anzac Pde, Doncaster Ave, High St, Alison Rd and Darley Rd as crowds travel to and from the event.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

Special event clearways will be in place at the following times and locations: 

  • 4am – 7pm: Darley Road between Alison Road and the Grand Drive roundabout (western side) 
  • 8am – 7pm: Ascot Street between Doncaster Avenue and the roundabout at the entry to Randwick Racecourse (both sides) 
  • 3pm – 7pm: High Street between Gate Two Avenue and Anzac Parade (southern side) 

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

roads.mainStreet: Anzac Parade, Alison Road, High Street

properties.publicTransport: L2/L3 light rail services will not operate between Circular Quay and Central due to maintenance. Match tickets include travel on transport. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
A-League Men: Sydney FC v Perth Glory at Allianz Stadium. Kick off from 7.35pm, full time at 9.20pm

Traffic will be heavier than usual in the area. Motorists should allow extra travel time on Anzac Pde, Lang Rd, Oxford St, Syd Einfeld Drive, Darley Rd, York Rd and Alison Rd.

  • Driver Avenue will be partially closed outside the stadium from 4.30pm and will reopen once crowds have safely cleared the area.  
  • No through-traffic will be available on Driver Avenue, however access to EP3 grass car park and drop-off areas maintained from Lang Road. Car Park open from 4.35pm.
  • Special event clearways will be in place on roads in Moore Park and surrounding areas from 3pm to 11pm on sections of Albion Street, Lang Road and Fitzroy Street and from 6am to 11pm on Cleveland Street.  

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator

arrangementElements.html:
Clearways are strict no-parking zones and also apply to local residents and Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs before parking as vehicles left in special event clearways will be towed and a towing fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator. 

From 6am to 11pm 

  • Cleveland Street between South Dowling Street and City Road (southern side) 
  • Cleveland Street between Abercrombie Street and South Dowling Street (northern side) 

From 3pm to 11pm 

  • Albion Street between Bourke Street and Flinders Street (both sides) 
  • Lang Road between Anzac Parade and Errol Flynn Boulevard (northern side) 
  • Fitzroy Street between South Dowling Street and Bourke Street (northern side)
arrangementElements.title: Special Event Clearways

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Wests Tigers v Broncos at Campbelltown Sports Stadium. Kick-off at 7.30pm, full time around 9.30pm.

Traffic may be heavier than usual as fans travel to the match. Allow extra travel time on roads including Pembroke Rd, Rose Payten Dr and Leumeah Rd.

Motorists should also be aware of increased pedestrian activity in the area.

roads.mainStreet: Pembroke Road and Rose Payten Drive

properties.publicTransport: Match tickets include travel on transport. Buses will divert while roads are closed after the game. Buses replace trains between Penrith, Richmond and Granville on T1 Western and T5 Cumberland Lines. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Eels v Bulldogs at CommBank Stadium in Parramatta. NRL kick-off at 4.05pm, full time around 5.50pm.

With thousands of fans expected, traffic will be heavy on roads approaching Parramatta and nearby car parks are expected to be in high demand.

Allow extra travel time on roads including Parramatta Road, Church Street, Victoria Road, James Ruse Drive and Pennant Hills Road. 

Motorists should also exercise caution as pedestrian activity will be high on roads between Parramatta Interchange and the stadium. 

Drivers are reminded that light rail services are running through Parramatta CBD. Trams can’t stop quickly or easily – so exercise extra caution when driving in the area. 

Road closures 

Roads around the stadium will close from 5.50pm until 6.20pm approx. to allow crowds to safely leave the venue. Roads will re-open once crowds have cleared the area. 

  • O’Connell Street from Grose Street to George Street 
  • Ross Street from O’Connell Street to Trott Street 
  • Victoria Road from O’Connell Street to Villiers Street 

While closures are in place motorists are encouraged to avoid driving through Parramatta CBD and detour around the closures via Grose Street, Brickfield Street, Victoria Road and Wilde Avenue in both directions.

properties.publicTransport: Match tickets include travel on public transport. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Rabbitohs v Dragons at Accor Stadium. NSW Cup from 3.10pm then NRL kick-off at 5.30pm. Full time around 7.15pm.

Motorists should allow extra travel time on roads including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.

If you need to drive, pre-book parking online in advance to secure a space. Follow the directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry. Note that P6 car park is closed due to Easter Show infrastructure.

Road closures and changes to parklands are in place within the Sydney Olympic Park Precinct for the Easter Show pack down. Check out the Sydney Olympic Park Authority interactive map for more details. For detailed closure and access changes in the precinct see the Olympic Park Community page.

arrangementElements.html:
Road closures and changes to parklands will be in place within the Sydney Olympic Park Precinct before, during and after the Easter Show for event set up and pack down. 

Check out the Sydney Olympic Park Authority interactive map for more details. For detailed closure and access changes in the precinct see the Olympic Park Community page. 

Monday 9 March at 1.30am to Tuesday 21 April at 1.30am:   

  • Olympic Boulevard 100m north of Dawn Fraser Avenue adjacent to Cathy Freeman Park amenities, to 200m south of Edwin Flack Avenue outside Qudos Bank Arena.  

Thursday 19 March at 1.30am to Tuesday 21 April at 1.30am:   

  • Australia Avenue from Murray Rose Avenue to Kevin Coombs Avenue. 
  • Showground Road from Murray Rose Avenue to Grand Parade. 
  • Grand Parade from Olympic Boulevard to Australia Avenue.
arrangementElements.title: Road closures

roads.mainStreet: M4 Motorway, Homebush Bay Drive, Parramatta Road, Centenary Drive

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Monster Truck Mania Live at Qudos Bank Arena in Sydney Olympic Park on Saturday 25 and Sunday 26 April. Show starts daily from 1pm

Traffic delays are expected as attendees make their way to Olympic Park each day. Motorists should allow extra travel time on roads including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.

Fans driving should pre-book parking online in advance to secure a space, available until 11:59pm the night before the show unless sold out prior. If you have pre-booked your parking, follow the directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry.

Road closures may be in place. Check out the Sydney Olympic Park Authority interactive map for more details.

roads.mainStreet: M4 Motorway, Homebush Bay Drive, Parramatta Road

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
A-League Men: Macarthur FC v Wellington Phoenix at Campbelltown Sports Stadium. Kick-off at 7.35pm, full time around 9.35pm.

Traffic may be heavier than usual as fans travel to and from the match during the busy evening peak. Allow extra travel time on roads including Pembroke Rd, Rose Payten Dr and Leumeah Rd.

Motorists should also be aware of increased pedestrian activity in the area.

roads.mainStreet: Pembroke Road and Rose Payten Drive

properties.publicTransport: Travel on rail services is included in tickets to the show. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Hawkesbury Show at Hawkesbury Showground. Starts daily at 9am, finish Friday and Saturday at 11pm and finish Sunday at 4pm.

Traffic will be heavy on approach to Hawkesbury Showground particularly along Richmond Rd, Windsor Rd, Hawkesbury Valley Way and The Northern Rd.

Motorists should expect delays and allow plenty of additional travel time.

Attendees should consider the public transport options to avoid delays and demand for parking.

properties.publicTransport: Shuttle bus services will operate for the NRL game only. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Upcoming events at McDonald Jones Stadium, Broadmeadow:

  • A-League Men Newcastle Jets v Central Coast Mariners on Saturday 25 April. Kick-off at 5pm
  • NRL: Knights v Panthers on Sunday 26 April. Kick-off at 2pm

Traffic may be heavier than usual as fans travel to and from the match. Allow extra travel time on Turton Rd, Griffiths Rd, Newcastle Rd, Lambton Rd and Bridges Rd through Broadmeadow while fans are making their way to and from the stadium.

For passenger drop-off, use the allocated 10-minute loading zone in the McDonald Jones Stadium car park. Entry is via the driveway off Turton Road.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.﻿

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Anzac Day races at Royal Randwick Racecourse.

Traffic will be heavier than usual on Anzac Pde, Doncaster Ave, High St, Alison Rd and Darley Rd as crowds travel to and from the event.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

roads.mainStreet: Anzac Parade, Alison Road, High Street

properties.publicTransport: Additional route 199 buses will also run towards Manly and Mona Vale after the game to help you get home. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Sea Eagles v Eels at 4 Pines Park in Brookvale. Prelim matches from 3.30pm. NRL kick-off at 4pm and full time around 6pm.

Traffic will be heavier than usual as fans travel to and from the match. Allow extra travel time on roads approaching the stadium, including Pittwater Rd, Old Pittwater Rd, Warringah Rd and Beacon Hill Rd.

Motorists should plan to arrive early as parking will be in high demand, and also be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

NOTE: There is no parking on the southern side of Federal Pde between Pine Ave and Alfred Rd permitted from 8pm the night before the match until the crowd has safely cleared the stadium after full time on game day.

properties.publicTransport: Match tickets include travel on transport. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
A-League: Western Sydney Wanderers v Melbourne Victory at CommBank Stadium. Kick-off at 7.35pm, full time at 9.30pm

Traffic will be heavier than usual on roads approaching the stadium as fans travel to the match. Allow extra travel time on roads including Parramatta Road, Church Street, Victoria Road, James Ruse Drive and Pennant Hills Road. 

Motorists should also exercise caution as pedestrian activity will be high on roads between Parramatta Interchange and the stadium. 

Be patient leaving car parks as traffic will be heavy and follow the directions of traffic controllers and Police.

properties.publicTransport: Route buses 333 and 380 will be diverting and missing stops while roads are closed. For more details check travel alerts at transportnsw.info.
properties.otherAdvice:
Roads in North Bondi will be closed for an Anzac Day Dawn Service to be held from 5.55am.

Closed from 4am to 7am:

  • Campbell Pde between Queen Elizabeth Dr and Brighton Blvd
  • Ramsgate Ave between Campbell Pde and North Bondi RSL

Motorists will be detoured via local roads. Allow extra travel time and follow the direction of traffic controllers onsite.

webLinks.linkText: Randwick City Council - Anzac Day, Coogee Dawn Service

properties.publicTransport: Route buses 313, 350, 370, 373 and 374 will be diverting while roads are closed. For more details check travel alerts at transportnsw.info.
properties.otherAdvice:
Road closures will be in place for an Anzac Day Dawn Service held at Goldstein Reserve, Coogee.

From 5am to 7.30am the following roads will close for the Dawn Service:

  • Arden St from Dolphin St to Carr St
  • Coogee Bay Rd from Vicar St to Arden St
  • Alfreda St at Arden St
  • Dolphin St, east from the roundabout on Arden St

The Dolphin Street car park (near Coogee Pavilion) will also be closed for the event from 5pm Friday 24 April to 7.30am Saturday 25 April.

Motorists can detour around the closures via local roads. Allow extra travel time and follow the direction of traffic controllers onsite.

roads.mainStreet: Arden Street, Coogee Bay Road, Alfreda Street

properties.otherAdvice:
Major road closures will be in place on Sunday 3 May between 4am and 1pm in the Sydney CBD, The Rocks, Barangaroo and Pyrmont for the Runaway Sydney Half Marathon. Half Marathon runners will be released in waves from 6.20am and the 10km event will start at 9.55am. Both courses start on the Cahill Expressway at Macquarie St near Circular Quay and will end at The Domain Sydney by 12.30pm.   

Motorists are encouraged to avoid non-essential travel in the CBD and Pyrmont areas during the event as delays are expected. If you must drive, diversions will be in place so allow extra trave time and follow detour signs. 

Key closures: 

  • Cahill Expressway Circular Quay Overpass – 4am to 12.30pm: closed in both directions between the Harbour Bridge and Domain Tunnel, including the on and off ramps at Macquarie St. Motorists travelling towards the Eastern Distributor/Eastern Suburbs should use the Sydney Harbour Tunnel while the Cahill Expressway Overpass is closed. 
  • Sydney CBD – 4am to 12.30pm: Macquarie Street will be closed from Albert St to Hyde Park. Motorists can cross Macquarie St at Shakespeare Pl / Bent St before 5.30am and after 12pm only. After 12pm, there is no access to or from Macquarie St; access to the Eastern Distributor will be via William St. Sections of Bridge St, Conservatorium Rd, Hunter St and Phillip Ln. Motorists leaving the city can access the Harbour Bridge via Clarence St or Kent St.  
  • Pyrmont, Barangaroo & King St Wharf – 5am to 10.30am: the Western Distributor will be closed from Pyrmont to the CBD, including the Fig St and Pyrmont St on-ramps and the King St off-ramp. There is NO direct access from the Anzac Bridge to the Sydney Harbour Bridge – citybound traffic on the Anzac Bridge will be detoured via the Bathurst St off-ramp. Citybound traffic from Pyrmont will be diverted via Harris St and local roads. Sections of Sussex St, Erskine St, Hickson Rd, Kent St, King St and other local streets will also be closed near King St Wharf and Barangaroo. Sections of Union St, Murray St, Pyrmont Bridge Rd, Pyrmont St, Darling Dr and surrounding local roads will be closed in Pyrmont.  
  • Dawes Point, Millers Point, The Rocks & Barangaroo – 5am to 12.30pm: Sections of Hickson Rd, Towns Pl, Dalgety Rd, Pottinger St, Windmill St, Watson Rd and surrounding streets will be closed. Access to The Rocks is maintained via York St, Lang St and Harrington St. 
  • Pyrmont – 5am to 11am: Sections of Pirrama Rd, Bowman St, Darling Island Rd, Harris St, Bank St, Refinery Dr and surrounding local roads will be closed.  
  • Pyrmont – 5am to 11.30am: Pirrama Rd between Jones Bay Rd and Murray St; and Murray St between Union St and Pirrama Rd will be closed. 
  • Sydney CBD and The Domain Sydney – from 5am: A section of College St will be closed until 9.30am; sections of Bent St, Shakespeare Pl and Sir John Young Cres will be closed from 5.30am to 12pm; and Art Gallery Rd, Hospital Rd, Mrs Macquaries Rd and Prince Albert Rd will be closed until 1pm.  

Special event clearway parking restrictions will be in effect from 2am on various roads throughout the Sydney CBD, The Domain Sydney, Circular Quay, The Rocks, Millers Point, Barangaroo and Pyrmont. All clearways will be lifted by 12.30pm and all roads will be reopened by 1pm.   

Special event clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

arrangementElements.html:
All closures affect both directions unless specified otherwise. 

SYDNEY CBD 

FROM 4AM TO 12.30PM 

  • Bridge St between Phillip St and Macquarie St 
  • Conservatorium Rd between Macquarie St and Government House gates 
  • Hunter St between Elizabeth St and Macquarie St 
  • Macquarie St* between Albert St and St James Rd 
  • Phillip Ln between Phillip St and Bent St 

*NOTE: Access to Sydney Opera House and Macquarie St north of Albert St is via Albert St. 

FROM 5AM TO 9.30AM 

  • College St between Prince Albert Rd and Park St (northbound) 

FROM 5AM TO 1PM 

  • Art Gallery Rd between Hospital Rd and Mrs Macquaries Rd 
  • Hospital Rd between Art Gallery Rd and Shakespeare Pl* 
  • Mrs Macquaries Rd from Art Gallery Rd (full loop) 
  • Prince Albert Rd between St Marys Rd and Hospital Rd 

*NOTE: Temporary two-way traffic arrangements in place on Hospital Rd from 5am to 1pm. Entry and exit via Prince Albert Rd only, under traffic control. 

CAHILL EXPRESSWAY / SYDNEY HARBOUR BRIDGE  

FROM 4AM TO 12.30PM 

  • Cahill Expressway between Conservatorium Rd and Bradfield Hwy (Circular Quay Overpass) 
  • Cahill Expressway from Domain Tunnel on-ramp 

NOTE: Access to Sydney Harbour Bridge from Sydney CBD is via Clarence St or Kent St.  

THE ROCKS / DAWES POINT / MILLERS POINT / WALSH BAY / BARANGAROO  

FROM 5AM TO 12.30PM 

  • Argyle Place between Dalgety Road and Lower Fort St 
  • Argyle St between Kent St and Lower Fort St 
  • Bettington St* between Dalgety Road and Merriman St 
  • Dalgety Rd between Towns Pl and Argyle Pl 
  • Hickson Rd between Napoleon St and Barton St (northbound) 
  • Hickson Rd between Barton St and OPT elevated roadway  
  • High Ln* between Argyle Pl, High St and Kent St 
  • High St* between Argyle Pl and Kent St 
  • Kent St* between High St and Argyle St (northbound) 
  • Kent St between Argyle St and Windmill St 
  • Lance Ln* between High St and High Ln 
  • Merriman St* from Bettington St 
  • Munn St from High St 
  • Pottinger St^ between Hickson Rd and Windmill St 
  • Rodens Ln* from Bettington St 
  • Towns Pl between Hickson Rd and Dalgety Rd 
  • Upper Fort St from Watson Rd  
  • Watson Rd between Upper Fort St and Argyle St 
  • Windmill St between Lower Fort St and Dalgety Road 
  • Windmill St (laneway) between Argyle Place and Windmill St  

NOTES: 

*Local access available to Kent St and High St as far north as Argyle St. After 11am, access to Dalgety Rd, Bettington St, Rodens Le, Merriman St and Barangaroo car park is via High St to Argyle Ple (west of Kent St only). Kent St, south of Argyle St, southbound exit allowed for local traffic only.   

^Exit via Windmill St only allowed from Pottinger St until 6am.   

WESTERN DISTRIBUTOR  

FROM 5AM to 10.30AM  

  • Fig St on-ramp to City and Sydney Harbour Bridge 
  • King St off-ramp to City North and Sydney Harbour Bridge 
  • Pyrmont St on-ramp to City and Sydney Harbour Bridge 

Western Distributor eastbound traffic will be diverted to the Bathurst St off-ramp: access to Sydney Harbour Bridge is via Bathurst St and Kent St then the Kent St on-ramp; access to CBD North is via Clarence St. 

SYDNEY CBD / BARANGAROO / PYRMONT  

FROM 5AM to 10.30AM  

  • Barton St between Barangaroo Ave and Hickson Rd 
  • Bunn St between Pyrmont St and Murray St 
  • Darling Dr** between Murray St and Iron Wharf Pl roundabout 
  • Edward Ln between Pyrmont St and Little Edward St 
  • Edward St between Pyrmont Bridge Rd and Union St 
  • Erskine St* between Kent St and Shelley St 
  • Hickson Rd between Napoleon St and Barton St (southbound) 
  • Kent St between Margaret St and Erskine St (southbound) 
  • King St between Sussex St and Kent St 
  • Margaret St between Clarence St and Kent St (westbound) 
  • Murray St^ between Pyrmont St and Union St (northbound) 
  • Napoleon St between Hickson Rd and Kent St 
  • Pyrmont Bridge Rd between Pyrmont St and Harris St (eastbound) 
  • Pyrmont Bridge Rd between Pyrmont St and Union St 
  • Pyrmont St between Pyrmont Bridge Rd and Murray St 
  • Slip St from Sussex St 
  • Sussex St between Napoleon St and King St 
  • Union St between Edward St and Murray St 
  • Watermans Quay between Barangaroo Ave and Hickson Road  

NOTES: 

*Between 6.30am and 10.30am:  

  • Local access to King St Wharf, Barangaroo South and Barangaroo Central precincts is via Harbour St to the Western Distributor northbound exit to Wheat Rd (sign-posted ‘Erskine St City North’)  
  • Restricted exit from King St Wharf and Barangaroo South and Central precincts via Wheat Rd under contra-flow for vehicles 7m or under in length (21 seat minibuses and smaller)  

^Local access maintained to Murray St south of Union St. NO access to Pyrmont Bridge Rd or Star Casino  

**Local access for residents and Ibis/ Novotel hotels only

CYCLING DETOUR – 5AM TO 12PM 

Cycling detours will be in place between Kent St cycleway and the Sydney Harbour Bridge cycleway, via Kent St Sydney Harbour Bridge on-ramp to Observatory Hill. Marshals will direct cyclists across Upper Fort St in both directions when safe gaps between runners are available. Alternatively, cyclists can travel between Sydney Harbour Bridge cycleway and the Sydney CBD via the “Bridge Stairs” Tunnel and through Cumberland St.

arrangementElements.title: Special Event Clearways

arrangementElements.html:
Motorists are advised to avoid King Street Wharf, Barangaroo and Crown Sydney precincts between 5am and 12.30pm other than for residential or essential access. Police may restrict vehicle access along closed roads, including to residents and businesses. 

Circular Quay 

  • From 4am, access to the Sydney Opera House and Macquarie Street (north of Albert Street) will be via Albert Street until Macquarie Street reopens at 12.30pm. 

Sydney Hospital & Hospital Road 

  • From 5am to 1pm, two-way access to Hospital Road is via Prince Albert Road only, under traffic control. No access from Shakespeare Place. 

King Street Wharf and Barangaroo South 

  • From 6.30am to 10.30am, access to and from King Street Wharf, Cockle Bay Wharf and Barangaroo South will be restricted to tenants/residents only. Access will be managed via Wheat Road from Harbour Street under traffic control; no access is available via Erskine Street or Sussex Street. 
  • Entry from the south via Harbour Street: continue northbound on the Western Distributor and veer left into Wheat Road at the signposted exit for Erskine Street/CBD North. 
  • Entry from Western Distributor: take the Bathurst Street exit towards Darling Harbour, turn right onto Sussex Street, right onto Liverpool Street and then Right onto Harbour Street and veer left into Wheat Road at the signposted exit for Erskine Street/CBD North. 
  • Exit the precinct via Wheat Road under contra-flow traffic control (light vehicles only). Vehicles over 7 metres (including trucks, buses and coaches) are unable to use the managed exit and will need to remain within the precinct until Erskine Street reopens. 
  • Motorists are strongly advised to avoid non-essential travel to or from these precincts between 6am and 10.30am as delays are expected. 
  • Before and after these times, access is maintained via Erskine Street at Kent Street. 

Millers Point & Barangaroo North 

  • Managed access to Kent Street and High Street properties as far north as Argyle Street is maintained throughout the event. Access via Kent Street northbound by traffic control. Kent Street southbound exit allowed for local traffic only. 
  • After 11am, access to Barangaroo Reserve & car park, Dalgety Road and Bettington Street will be available via Kent Street, High Street, Argyle Place and Dalgety Road until roads reopen at 12.30pm. 
  • Pottinger Street exit permitted via Windmill Street until 6am. 

The Rocks & Dawes Point  

  • Access maintained via Lang Street and Harrington Street throughout the event. 

Pyrmont 

  • Access to Murray Street properties/hotels and surrounding local roads permitted via Darling Drive under traffic control for light vehicles and coaches only (trucks not permitted). Note, coaches can only turn around via hotel driveways. 
  • No access available to Pyrmont Bridge Road or Star Casino from 5am to 10.30am. Access for taxis to Star Casino maintained via Jones Bay Road. 
  • Refinery Drive will be closed from 5am to 11am with exit permitted via Tambua Street before 6.45am and after 10.45am, only at Police discretion when there are safe gaps between runners. If you need access to your vehicle/property during these times, please plan ahead and relocate your vehicle prior. 
  • Access to and from The Star car park is via Pyrmont Street. 
  • No access from/to Pirrama Road from Wharf 7 and Wharf 10 access roads from 5am to 11.30am. 
  • No access to Murray Street from the Australian National Maritime Museum access road from 5am to 11.30am. 
  • Exit from driveways on Pyrmont Street properties between Bunn Lane and Murray Street permitted southbound under traffic control throughout the event. 
  • Resident access maintained to Bowman Street. Exit for local traffic via John Street only.
arrangementElements.title: Managed Local Access Arangements

properties.diversions: Travel on transport is included for participants. Buses in the city and Pyrmont will be diverting and using different stops while road closures are in place. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.

roads.mainStreet: Cahill Expressway, Western Distributor, Macquarie Street, Hickson Road, The Domain Sydney

properties.otherAdvice:
Princes Hwy will be closed for 10 minutes in both directions between Shore St and Mirrabooka Ave for Anzac Day commemorations.

Motorists are advised to plan their journey and allow extra travel time.

Other local road closures:

·        Mirrabooka Ave (between Page St and the Ambulance station) from 5am to 6.30am and 10.45am to 11.30am

·        Page St (between Campbell St and Queen St) from 5am to 6.30am and 10.45am to 11.30am

·        Shore St (west of Princes Hwy) from 10.30am to 11.30am

Princes Hwy will be closed in both directions between Field St and Bowen St for approximately 10 minutes for Anzac Day commemorations.

Motorists are advised to plan their journey and allow extra travel time.

Orient St (Old Princes Hwy) will be closed from Flora Cres to Clyde St from 8am to 11:30am for Anzac Day commemorations.

Re-opening times will stagger depending when the March has moved on. At this stage Orient St, North St and Flora Crescent are expected to re-open at 11am.

Motorists are advised to take extra caution in the area as there is an expected increase in pedestrian activity.

properties.publicTransport: With Maitland Railway Station being the main hub of the event. Getting here via train is the easiest way to get to Steamfest.
properties.otherAdvice:
Up to 60,000 people are expected to attend the Hunter Valley Steamfest event at Maitland Station over the weekend of 18 and 19 April 2026.

Traffic is expected to be heavy approaching Maitland especially along Cessnock Road, New England Highway, Les Darcy Drive, High Street, Allan Walsh Drive and Ken Tubman Drive.

Motorists are advised to expect delays in the area and follow detours and direction of traffic controllers in the area.
properties.diversions: Local road closures and diversions will be in place.

webLinks.linkText: NSW Government - Anzac Day in NSW

properties.publicTransport: Travel on public transport is free on Anzac Day for veterans and families. Additional early morning services will run. Buses will be diverting while roads are closed. Light rail services will not run between Town Hall and Circular Quay until 6.15am due to the Dawn Service, and may be temporarily held at stops or partially closed between 12.30pm and 1.30pm due to the March. Visit transportnsw.info for details.
properties.otherAdvice:
Major roads will be closed in the Sydney CBD for the Anzac Day Dawn Service in Martin Place from 4.20am and for the March on Elizabeth St from 9am.  

Motorists should avoid travelling through the CBD while roads are closed or expect delays and allow extra travel time. While roads are closed travel east/west across the CBD is maintained via Bridge St or via Goulburn St. 

Key road closures:  

  • From 12am to 8am: Macquarie St north of Albert St.  
  • From 1am to 3pm: Pitt St and Castlereagh St between Hunter St and King St. A westbound section of Hunter St between Elizabeth St and Hamilton St. Hosking Pl from Castlereagh St.  
  • From 1am to 6pm: Angel Pl and Ash St.  
  • From 6am to 3pm: Major roads between Bridge St in Circular Quay and Goulburn St in the CBD South, including sections of Bathurst St, Bent St, Bligh St, Castlereagh St, Elizabeth St, Gresham St, Hunter St, King St, Liverpool St, Loftus St, Market St, O’Connell St, Park St, Phillip St, Pitt St and St James Rd. 

There will be no managed vehicle access or access to/from driveways within the road closure footprint for the safety of participants and spectators. Pedestrians can cross the March route at the designated crossing point on Elizabeth St at Park St and Hunter St, and at underground crossings at Bathurst St, Museum Station and Martin Place Station. 

Cycleway closures will be in place on Pitt St from 1am to 3pm, and on sections of Castlereagh St, King St, Liverpool St, Park St, Pitt St and Phillip St from 6am to 3pm. 

Extensive special event clearway parking restrictions will be in effect on various major roads in the Sydney CBD with some starting from 10pm on Friday 24 April. Clearways will be in effect from 1am around Martin Place for the Dawn Service, and from 5.30am on all other major roads.   

All major road closures and clearways are due to be lifted by 3pm.  

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types and apply even to local residents, businesses or TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies so check signs carefully on the day and the night before as clearways come into effect in the early morning. If your vehicle is towed, you can search for your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

properties.publicTransport: Routes 441, 504 and 506 will be diverting while road closures are in place. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Thousands of participants will take part in the 5km and 10km Mother's Day Classic fun run and walk events starting from 7am in The Domain. Participants will travel along sections of Macquarie St, College St then Art Gallery Rd and Mrs Macquarie's Rd to finish back in The Domain by approximately 12pm.

Mother's Day is traditionally one of the busiest days on the roads. Wherever you are travelling, expect it to take longer than usual and factor in extra travel time. 

ROAD CLOSURES

  • 5am to 12pm midday: Art Gallery Rd/Mrs Macquarie's Rd from Prince Albert Rd. 
  • 5am to 10.30am: Hospital Rd and a section of Prince Albert Rd. Access to Sydney Hospital and Hospital Rd is via Shakespeare Pl only in both directions.
  • 6am to 10.30am: Macquarie St between St James Rd and Hunter St, College St between Park St and Prince Albert Rd and sections of St James Rd, Prince Albert Rd, St Mary's Rd and Yurong Pkwy will be closed.

Special event clearways will be in effect from 5am to 10.30am on sections of Macquarie St, Hunter St, College St, Prince Albert Rd, St Mary's Rd and St James Rd. Clearways will also be in effect on sections of Prince Albert Rd and Hospital Rd from 5am to 12pm midday.

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

roads.mainStreet: Macquarie St, Prince Albert Rd, College St, Art Gallery Rd

properties.otherAdvice: Traffic controllers will hold traffic on Sturt Hwy at Court St and We St with traffic held whilst the march passes through.

The Mid Western Hwy (Adelaide St) from Burns St to Church St will be closed from 10:30am to 1:30pm for Anzac Day commemorations.

Milthorpe/Orange Rd (Church St) from Adelaide St to Lindsay St will also be closed. Motorists should allow extra travel time and follow the directions of traffic controllers on-site.
properties.diversions:
Eastbound detour via Church Street > Lindsay Street > Ogilvy Street.

Westbound detour via Burns Street > Henry Street > Martin Street > Adelaide Street > Water Street.

properties.otherAdvice:
Great Western Highway will be closed in both directions between Hare Street and Ross Street for up to 15 minutes between 10:35am and 10:50am.

There will be no access to Hare Street from Great Western Highway between 5:15am and 11:45am. 

There will be no access to Ross Street from Great Western Highway between 10:00am and 10:50am.
Westbound left turn lane from the Great Western Highway into Honour Avenue will be between 4:30am to 12:30pm. Through traffic on the Great Western Highway is not affected.
Oberon St between Fleming St and Dudley St will be closed from 10am to 11am for ANZAC Day commemorations.

properties.otherAdvice:
Runway Run/Walk at Western Sydney International Airport.

The Runway Run/Walk event gives runners and walkers the unique opportunity to take part in various distance events on the airport runway before it welcomes its first passengers and planes later this year. Runs start from 7am, with the community walk finishing by 4.30pm.

With a sold out group of 20,000 runners and walkers expected, attendees are urged to plan their travel ahead of time, and motorists should expect delays on roads approaching the precinct.

Parking onsite is available to participants who have reserved a spot in advance only.

Access to the Airport is via Warami Dr from the M12 Motorway or Elizabeth Dr.

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Upcoming shows at the TikTok Entertainment Centre:

  • Friday 17 April: Guo Degang & Yuqian Cross Talk Show from 7.30pm to 10.30pm
  • Sunday 19 April: ITZY from 7pm to 10pm
  • Thursday 30 April to Saturday 2 May: Guy Sebastian. Shows start at 7pm nightly.

Traffic may be heavier than usual as attendees travel to and from the event.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

The Grafton Show will be held on Friday 9 and Saturday 25 April at the Grafton Showground from 9am to 9pm. Traffic control will be in place on Villiers Street to allow safe pedestrian access. Motorists are advised to exercise caution24

properties.publicTransport: Travel on transport is included in match tickets and additional light rail services will run. L2 & L3 light rail services will not operate from Circular Quay to Central due to maintenance. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Roosters v Knights at Allianz Stadium. Kick-off at 2pm, full time at 3.50pm.

Traffic delays are expected on roads approaching Moore Park as fans make their way to the precinct

Motorists should carpool and allow plenty of extra travel time on roads including the Eastern Distributor, Moore Park Rd, Cleveland St, Anzac Pde and Lang Rd.

Changed traffic conditions:

  • Driver Avenue will be partially closed outside the stadium from 11am and will reopen once crowds have safely cleared the area.   
  • No through-traffic will be available on Driver Avenue, however access to EP3 grass car park and drop-off areas will be maintained from Lang Road. Grass car park open from 11am.   
  • Special event clearways will be in place on roads in Moore Park and surrounding areas from 11am to 6pm on sections of Albion Street, Lang Road and Fitzroy Street, and from 6am to 7pm on sections of Cleveland Street

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

Clearways are strict no-parking zones and also apply to local residents and Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs before parking as vehicles left in special event clearways will be towed and a towing fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator. 

From 6am to 7pm 

  • Cleveland Street between South Dowling Street and City Road (southern side) 
  • Cleveland Street between Abercrombie Street and South Dowling Street (northern side) 

From 11am to 6pm 

  • Albion Street between Bourke Street and Flinders Street (both sides) 
  • Lang Road between Anzac Parade and Errol Flynn Boulevard (northern side)
  • Fitzroy Street between South Dowling Street and Bourke Street (northern side)

properties.publicTransport: Travel on transport is included in match tickets and additional light rail and shuttle bus services will run. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Double header at Moore Park:

  • Super Rugby: Waratahs v Pasifika at Allianz Stadium. Kick-off at 7.35pm, full time at 9.20pm.
  • AFL: Sydney Swans v GWS Giants at the SCG. VFL first bounce at 3.35pm, AFL first bounce at 7.50pm and full time at 10.30pm

Moore Park will be very busy on Friday night with 50,000 AFL and Super Rugby fans travelling to the precinct for matches at neighbouring stadiums kicking off around the same time. Traffic delays are expected on roads approaching Moore Park as rugby fans make their way to the precinct in the already busy evening peak.

Motorists should carpool and allow plenty of extra travel time on roads including the Eastern Distributor, Moore Park Rd, Cleveland St, Anzac Pde and Lang Rd.

Changed traffic conditions:

  • Driver Avenue will be partially closed outside the stadiums from 3.30pm and will reopen once crowds have safely cleared the area.   
  • No through-traffic will be available on Driver Avenue, however access to EP2 Kippax car park will be maintained via Moore Park Road and access to EP3 grass car park and drop-off areas maintained from Lang Road. Car Parks open from 4.45pm. 
  • Special event clearways will be in place on roads in Moore Park and surrounding areas from 3pm to midnight on sections of Albion Street, Chalmers St, Moore Park Road and Lang Road, and from 6am to midnight on sections of Cleveland Street and Fitzroy Street. 

 Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.
